untranslatable_reason: This signal requires options-specific data (in-the-money status, parity pricing, discount-to-parity calculation, days-to-expiration) that are not available in the allowed indicator set. The rule operates on options market microstructure and assignment probability, not on the underlying asset's OHLCV price action. Standard equity price indicators cannot model option parity, moneyness, or time decay dynamics.
translation_notes: Signal MCMILLAN_DRY_230 is an event-based options assignment warning system from McMillan's trading book. It requires real-time options chain data (strike prices, bid-ask spreads, time to expiration) to detect when ITM calls trade below intrinsic value—a signal of imminent assignment. This cannot be translated to a price-action backtest engine without options data feeds.
